new_rules.rule: Fallback loaders for shared runtime modules must choose candidates by successful loadability in the current runtime, not by file existence alone, and must continue to the next candidate when a preferred format is unsupported.
applies_to: dynamic import bridges between source and dist
runtime bootstrap for shared governed modules
new_rules.do: In a loader that prefers source `engine.ts` but can fall back to built `engine.js`, wrap the import itself in try/catch; if importing the `.ts` file fails with an unsupported-format/runtime-loader error, continue to `shared/dist/.../engine.js` and only throw after all candidates are exhausted or a selected candidate loads and then fails internally.
new_rules.dont: Check `access('shared/review-engine/engine.ts')` and immediately `import()` it because the file exists, then abort on an unsupported `.ts` import error without ever trying `shared/dist/review-engine/engine.js`.
new_rules.source: Round 10: source/dist shared-module fallback stopped at an unsupported source candidate instead of reaching the canonical runtime module.

new_rules.rule: Canonical machine-facing artifacts must be rewritten from the current state on every update path, even when the new collection is empty, so stale prior artifacts cannot persist as false current truth.
new_rules.do: When rewriting `fix-items-map.json`, always write the current schema object after repair, including `items: []` when nothing remains, so downstream readers can distinguish 'resolved to empty' from 'file missing' or stale prior contents.
new_rules.dont: Skip writing the artifact when the new items array is empty, leaving yesterday's non-empty `fix-items-map.json` on disk and causing later consumers to treat old findings as still active.
new_rules.source: Round 10: zero-item ultimatum output left a stale canonical fix-items artifact on disk.

existing_rules_covering.explanation: This is already the cross-field invariant rule: leader status must be legal for the actual unresolved severity set before it drives workflow state. Treating raw `pushback` as proof of material work instead of validating against blocking/major items is exactly the illegal status/output combination COD-005 is meant to catch and override.

The generalized failure is already captured: prompt text was transported through shell command-line construction instead of a safe non-shell stdin/file boundary. The fact that it lives in a mirrored Codex invoker is an instance of the same shell-argument rule violation, even if the mirror drift also has a secondary copy-maintenance aspect.

The terminal `resume_required` reason and deferred-work count should be computed from the canonical post-ultimatum result on that exit path. Reporting a pre-ultimatum material count in the final status reason is a terminal-summary derivation mismatch, which COD-009 already forbids.

This is directly the single-canonical-implementation rule. Keeping a second `review_issue_count` computation in another module, even if logically aligned today, preserves duplicate live workflow logic and reintroduces drift risk that COD-004 was written to eliminate.
